title: astragalus angustifolius
intro: Astragalus angustifolius, also known as the narrowleaf milkvetch, is a perennial legume native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of isoflavones, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects. Astragalus angustifolius can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is often used to support immune function, reduce fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
